equations.raw: # Delta_L = (scalar Laplacian) tensor I_35 + I_{d^2} tensor R_endo + I_{d^2} tensor Ric_endo
equations.context: For our purposes, the crucial observation is that the Lichnerowicz operator full action on sector (p,q) decomposes as:

equations.raw: nabla^* nabla h = scalar_lap tensor I_35 + fiber_connection_terms
equations.context: The mixed connection terms come from nabla acting on the TENSOR INDICES. For the Weitzenbock decomposition:

equations.raw: # For a bi-invariant metric (tau=0), the fiber connection terms vanish
equations.context: For the Weitzenbock decomposition: nabla^* nabla h = scalar_lap tensor I_35 + fiber_connection_terms

equations.raw: C_{bc, de} = 2*sum_a Gamma^d_{ab} delta_{ec} + 2*sum_a Gamma^e_{ac} delta_{db}
equations.context: The fiber connection correction:

equations.raw: (nabla_{e_a} h)_{bc} = rho_ON[a] h_{bc}  [acting on function index]
equations.context: We work with FULL n*n tensor, then project to Sym^2_0. Full tensor h_{bc}: index flattened as (b*n + c).

equations.raw: nabla_a acting on psi = phi tensor h (phi in V_pi, h in R^{n^2}):
equations.context: In matrix form for the covariant derivative on full V_pi x R^{n^2}:

equations.raw: (nabla_a psi)_{I, bc} = (rho_ON[a] phi)_I h_{bc}
equations.context: In matrix form for the covariant derivative on full V_pi x R^{n^2}: nabla_a acting on psi = phi tensor h (phi in V_pi, h in R^{n^2}):

equations.raw: # As a (d * n^2) x (d * n^2) matrix (outer index = fiber slot (b,c)):
equations.context: - sum_d Gamma[d,a,b] phi_I h_{dc} - sum_d Gamma[d,a,c] phi_I h_{bd}

equations.raw: = rho_ON[a][I,J] * delta_{be} * delta_{cf}
equations.context: As a (d * n^2) x (d * n^2) matrix (outer index = fiber slot (b,c)): nabla_a[I*n^2 + b*n + c, J*n^2 + e*n + f]:

equations.raw: Index layout: psi[I, b, c] with total index = I*n^2 + b*n + c
equations.context: Build covariant derivative operators on the full tensor space

equations.raw: = rho_ON[a] tensor I_{n^2}
equations.context: Term 1: rho_ON[a] on V_pi part: I_d tensor (I_n tensor I_n) ... actually rho_ON[a] acts on the d-index (I) while leaving (b,c) alone

equations.raw: In flat index: source = I*n^2 + b*n + c, target = I*n^2 + d*n + c
equations.context: Term 2: -Gamma^d_{ab} on first tensor index b -> d Acts as: psi[I, b, c] -> -Gamma[d,a,b] psi[I, d, c] for all I,c

equations.raw: For all I and c: mat[I*n^2 + dd*n + c, I*n^2 + b*n + c] -= g_val * delta_{IJ}
equations.context: g_val = Gamma[dd, a, b] if abs(g_val) > 1e-15:

equations.raw: Rough Laplacian = -sum_a nabla_a^2 + sum_c div_c * nabla_c
equations.context: div[c] += Gamma[c, a, a]

equations.raw: P[I, b*n+c] = basis[I, b, c], shape (35, n^2)
equations.context: Project to Sym^2_0 fiber:

equations.raw: P_full = I_d tensor P, shape (d*35, d*n^2)
equations.context: Full projection to V_{pq} tensor Sym^2_0:

equations.raw: R_endo is (35,35), R_endo_full = I_d tensor R_endo (d*35, d*35)
At tau=0: ALL 35 Sym^2_0 tensors are TT (bi-invariant => nabla=0 => div=0).
(div h)_b = sum_a [nabla_a h]_{ab}
equations.context: The divergence of a symmetric 2-tensor in ON frame:

equations.raw: = sum_a [e_a(h_{ab}) - Gamma^c_{aa} h_{cb} - Gamma^c_{ab} h_{ac}]
equations.context: The divergence of a symmetric 2-tensor in ON frame: (div h)_b = sum_a [nabla_a h]_{ab}

equations.raw: (D psi)_{I, b} = sum_a sum_{c,d} (rho_ON[a])_{IJ} basis[K,a,b] psi[J,K]
equations.context: Divergence matrix D: (d*8) x (d*35)

equations.raw: TT subspace = kernel of div_mat
equations.context: div_mat: (d*n, d*35) complex matrix

equations.raw: Delta_L_TT = TT_basis @ Delta_L_full @ TT_basis^H
equations.context: Project Lichnerowicz to TT subspace

equations.raw: div_mat[(I*n+b), (J*35+K)] += rho_ON[a][I,J] * basis[K,a,b]
equations.context: Term 1: rho_ON[a][I,J] * basis[K, a, b] Outer product: (rho_ON[a]) tensor diag(basis[:, a, b])

equations.raw: For each K: div_mat[:, (J*35+K)] += rho_ON[a][:, J] * coeff1[K]
equations.context: div_mat[(I*n+b), (J*35+K)] += rho_ON[a][I,J] * basis[K,a,b] coeff1 = basis[:, a, b]  (35,) vector of coefficients

equations.raw: Block: row-block = b (fixed), col-block = K (varies)
equations.context: coeff1 = basis[:, a, b]  (35,) vector of coefficients For each K: div_mat[:, (J*35+K)] += rho_ON[a][:, J] * coeff1[K]

equations.raw: For each I,J: div_mat[I*n+b, J*35+K] += rho_ON[a][I,J] * val
equations.context: val = coeff1[K] if abs(val) > 1e-15:

equations.raw: -delta_{IJ} * val at row=(I,b), col=(J,K) with I==J
equations.context: val = div_trace[dd] * basis[K, dd, b] if abs(val) > 1e-15:

equations.raw: The div-free condition removes dim(adjoint) = 8 modes, leaving 35 - 8 = 27.
equations.context: TT condition: sym^2_0 AND div-free. At bi-invariant, the traceless condition is already imposed.

equations.raw: At tau=0 (bi-invariant), ALL of Sym^2_0 is TT because constant tensors
are parallel (nabla=0 => div=0). So TT dim = 35, not 27.
equations.context: At tau=0 (bi-invariant), ALL of Sym^2_0 is TT because constant tensors

equations.raw: At tau=0, the Lichnerowicz on (0,0) gives eigenvalues 1/3 (x27) and 3/4 (x8).
equations.context: if len(evals_TT) > 0: print(f"  (0,0) TT eigenvalues: min={np.min(evals_TT):.6f}, max={np.max(evals_TT):.6f}")

equations.raw: The 27-dim block gives m^2 = 1/3 - R_K/4 = 1/3 - 1/2 = -1/6 (tachyonic).
equations.context: print(f"  (0,0) TT eigenvalues: min={np.min(evals_TT):.6f}, max={np.max(evals_TT):.6f}") At tau=0, the Lichnerowicz on (0,0) gives eigenvalues 1/3 (x27) and 3/4 (x8).

equations.raw: CW: |lambda|^2 = Laplacian eigenvalue, so CW weight = |lambda|^4 log(|lambda|^2)
equations.context: mults = data['multiplicities'][idx] E_fermion[i] = 0.5 * np.sum(mults * evals)
